chunks.text: MAK/PS ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Const. Petition No. D-3190 of 2025 __________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature(s) of Judge(s) Priority 1. For orders on office objections. 2. For hearing of Misc. No.13783/25 3. For hearing of Misc. No.13795/25 4. For hearing of main case. 11.08.2025. M/s. Naveed Ahmed Khan and Farooq Abbasi, Advocates for the Petitioner. Ms. Wajeeha M. Mehdi, Assistant Attorney General. ------------------------------ Learned counsel for the petitioner has sought directions for Respondent No.2 & 3 being Pakistan Bar Council and Directorate of Legal Education to issue No Objection Certificate (NOC) to the petitioner so that law faculty run its academic session. Pakistan Bar Council has been in communication with the Petitioner’s University for quite sometime. This is second show cause notice dated 21.07.2023 whereby following orders were passed: “Pursuant to the above decision you are requested to please SHOW CAUSE that under which authority your University has granted admission to students in LL.B program without recognition of the Pakistan Bar Council and NOC of the Higher Education Commission and why penalty of Rupees 4 million shall not be imposed upon your University for violating Rules of this Council. Furthermore, fresh admission in LL.B. Program of Metropolitan University, Karachi has been banned. You are, therefore, requested to send the reply of Show Cause and above information / documents to this office at the earliest.” This matter is currently pending before the Pakistan Bar Council. Accordingly, that proceedings should be taken to its logical conclusion before this Court exercises its constitutional jurisdiction.

You are, therefore, requested to send the reply of Show Cause and above information / documents to this office at the earliest.” This matter is currently pending before the Pakistan Bar Council. Accordingly, that proceedings should be taken to its logical conclusion before this Court exercises its constitutional jurisdiction. Therefore, the instant petition is dismissed, along with all pending applications. HEAD OF CONST. BENCHES JUDGE
